mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and lowers borrowing costs, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals deteriorating household income and collapsing aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in real retail sales and accelerating consumer price inflation characterizes a stagflationary demand contraction that compresses real corporate revenues and triggers monetary restraint, jointly driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction term remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal does not produce negative returns in more than 55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals rising manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization during early-cycle recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship vanishes when the economy operates at full employment.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of early-cycle months with accelerating INDPRO, or the signal Sharpe ratio is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in new private housing starts signals expanding construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of months with rising HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in real retail sales below its 6-month trailing level confirms a consumer demand contraction that reduces corporate top-line growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production indicates a broad-based output and employment contraction that undermines corporate earnings across sectors, jointly driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of concurrent downturns, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating above 4% annualized over a 3-month window forces anticipated Federal Reserve tightening, raising real discount rates and depressing near-term equity valuations.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered inflation-spike episodes over a 5-year window.
